title: Risk Assessment Engine
description: Core risk assessment engine for evaluating documents against export control regulations. Includes risk scoring algorithm, configurable thresholds, risk category classification, and trigger paragraph extraction.
acceptance_criteria: Documents receive a risk score between 0-100%
Risk categories (Restricted, High, Medium, Low, Minimal) are assigned based on configurable thresholds
Trigger paragraphs are extracted and highlighted in results
Risk scoring works for all supported document types

title: Review Queue
description: Document review queue workflow enabling analysts to submit, claim, review, and disposition documents flagged for export control review.
acceptance_criteria: Documents can be submitted to the review queue after evaluation
Reviewers can claim, approve, decline, or release documents
Complete audit trail of all review actions
Auto-retrieve mode for efficient batch review

title: User Management & Auth
description: Multi-user authentication system with role-based access control. Supports Administrator, Master Analyst, Analyst, Export Control, and User roles with configurable permissions.
acceptance_criteria: Secure login with bcrypt password hashing
Five distinct roles with configurable permissions
24-hour rolling sessions with multi-tab support
Admin can create, update, and delete users

title: LLM Integration
description: Integration layer for AI/LLM providers to power document risk assessment. Currently supports Anthropic Claude and OpenAI with model selection and connection testing.
acceptance_criteria: Users can select between supported LLM providers
Model selection within each provider
Connection testing before evaluation
Graceful fallback on API errors

title: Document Processing
description: Document ingestion and text extraction pipeline supporting PDF, DOC, DOCX, TXT, PPTX, and XLSX/XLS formats. Includes batch processing with progress tracking.
acceptance_criteria: Support for PDF, DOC, DOCX, TXT, PPTX, XLSX file types
Batch file upload with progress tracking
Duplicate file detection
Document preview with slide-by-slide rendering for presentations

title: Regulation Library
description: Management system for regulation documents used as reference material during export control assessments. Includes document upload, version control, and status tracking.
acceptance_criteria: CRUD operations for regulation documents
Version control with document history
Active/Inactive status management
Regulation content available to AI prompts during evaluation

title: Deployment & DevOps
description: Infrastructure, deployment automation, and operational tooling for ERIS. Covers PM2 configuration, deployment scripts, SSL setup, and multi-environment support.
acceptance_criteria: Automated deployment via shell scripts
PM2 process management in production
SSL/HTTPS support with auto-renewal
Multi-environment support (DEV/QA/UAT)

title: Implement risk scoring algorithm with weighted category analysis
description: Implement the core risk scoring algorithm that analyzes document content against export control criteria and produces a 0-100% risk score. The algorithm should weight different risk indicators based on configurable category rules.
acceptance_criteria: Risk score is calculated as a percentage between 0-100
Score considers multiple risk indicators with configurable weights
Algorithm handles edge cases: empty documents, very short text, non-English content
Scoring results are deterministic for the same input and model

title: Add configurable risk thresholds for category classification
description: Allow administrators to configure the score thresholds that determine risk category boundaries (Restricted, High, Medium, Low, Minimal). Thresholds are stored in risk-category-rules.json and editable via the settings UI.
acceptance_criteria: Admin can view and edit risk threshold values in settings
Changes persist in risk-category-rules.json
Validation prevents overlapping or invalid threshold ranges
Changes take effect immediately for new evaluations

title: Risk score shows NaN for empty documents
description: When a user submits an empty document or a document with no extractable text (e.g., image-only PDF without OCR), the risk score displays as NaN instead of handling the edge case gracefully.
acceptance_criteria: Empty documents return a score of 0 with a 'no content' warning
Image-only PDFs display a message indicating no text was extracted
NaN is never displayed to the user

title: Refactor risk category rules configuration to use database
description: Move risk category rules from the JSON file (risk-category-rules.json) to the SQLite database for better consistency and to support future multi-tenancy requirements.
acceptance_criteria: Risk rules stored in SQLite table with migration script
API endpoints updated to read/write from database
Backward compatibility with existing JSON config during migration
Rollback procedure documented

title: Add risk score trend analysis for repeat documents
description: Track risk score changes over time when the same document is re-evaluated. Display a trend indicator showing whether risk increased, decreased, or remained stable across evaluations.
acceptance_criteria: System identifies previously evaluated documents by hash
Score history is stored and retrievable
UI shows trend arrow (up/down/stable) next to current score
Trend data is available in assessment log export

title: Risk category label misaligned on mobile viewport
acceptance_criteria: Risk category badge is properly aligned on screens < 768px
No text overflow or truncation on category labels

title: Implement document submission to review queue
description: Allow users to submit evaluated documents to the review queue for analyst review. The submission should capture the document content, evaluation results, and submitter information.
acceptance_criteria: Submit button appears after evaluation completes
Submission captures document, risk score, and evaluation metadata
Submitted documents appear in the review queue with 'pending' status
Duplicate submission prevention for the same document

title: Implement claim-based document assignment for reviewers
description: Reviewers can claim documents from the queue to prevent double-review. A claimed document is locked to the claiming reviewer until they approve, decline, or release it.
acceptance_criteria: Reviewer can claim an unclaimed document
Claimed documents show the reviewer's name and claim timestamp
Other reviewers cannot claim an already-claimed document
Reviewer can release a document back to the queue

title: Add review queue filtering and sorting
description: Enable reviewers to filter the review queue by status, risk category, date range, and submitter. Add sorting by priority, date submitted, and risk score.
acceptance_criteria: Filter by status: pending, under_review, approved, declined
Filter by risk category
Sort by date submitted, risk score, or priority
Filters persist across page navigation

title: Review queue count badge shows stale data after action
description: After approving or declining a document, the review queue count badge in the sidebar does not update until the page is manually refreshed. The badge should reflect the current count in real-time.
acceptance_criteria: Badge updates immediately after approve/decline/release actions
Badge count matches actual pending document count

title: Add auto-retrieve mode for sequential document review
description: Implement auto-retrieve functionality that automatically fetches the next document in the queue after a reviewer completes an action (approve/decline). This speeds up batch review sessions.
acceptance_criteria: Toggle switch to enable/disable auto-retrieve
Next document loads automatically after approve or decline
If queue is empty, display appropriate message
Auto-retrieve preference persists in user session

title: Add review queue dashboard with summary statistics
description: Create a dashboard view for the review queue showing aggregate statistics: total pending, approved today, declined today, average review time, and distribution by risk category.

title: Implement user login with bcrypt password hashing
description: Implement secure user authentication using bcrypt for password hashing. Users authenticate with username and password, receiving a session cookie on success.
acceptance_criteria: Passwords are hashed with bcrypt (10 salt rounds)
Login returns session cookie on success
Failed login returns generic error (no username enumeration)
Account lockout after 5 consecutive failed attempts

title: Implement role-based access control with five user roles
description: Define and enforce five user roles: Administrator, Master Analyst, Analyst, Export Control, and User. Each role has specific permissions configured in access-permissions.json. The Administrator role has full access, while other roles have progressively restricted permissions.
acceptance_criteria: Five roles defined: Administrator, Master Analyst, Analyst, Export Control, User
Permissions configurable via access-permissions.json
Middleware enforces role checks on all protected endpoints
Role changes take effect on next login

title: Implement user CRUD operations for admin panel
acceptance_criteria: Admin can create users with username, password, and role
Admin can update user details and role
Admin can delete users (except own account)
User list displays all users with role and last login

title: Session cookie not cleared properly on logout in Safari
description: On Safari browsers, the session cookie persists after logout, allowing the user to access protected pages briefly before the server rejects the invalid session. The cookie path may not match the BASE_PATH configuration.
acceptance_criteria: Session cookie is properly cleared on all major browsers
Cookie path matches BASE_PATH configuration
No authenticated pages accessible after logout

title: Add password complexity requirements and expiration policy
description: Enforce password complexity rules (minimum length, uppercase, number, special character) and implement a 90-day password expiration policy with advance warning notifications.
acceptance_criteria: Passwords must be at least 12 characters with mixed case, number, and special character
Expired passwords force a change on next login
Warning shown 14 days before expiration
Last 5 passwords cannot be reused

title: Admin can delete their own account causing system lockout
acceptance_criteria: API prevents deletion of the currently logged-in admin user
UI disables delete button for own account
System always has at least one administrator

title: Implement LLM provider selection and model configuration
description: Allow users to select between Anthropic Claude and OpenAI as their LLM provider, choose specific models within each provider, and test the connection before running evaluations.
acceptance_criteria: Provider dropdown with Anthropic and OpenAI options
Model list updates based on selected provider
Connection test validates API key and model access
Selected configuration persists in llm-settings.json

title: Implement custom prompt management per user
description: Each user can customize the AI prompt used for their evaluations. The prompt is stored per-user and falls back to the global prompt if not customized.
acceptance_criteria: Users can view, edit, and save their personal prompt
Default prompt falls back to global prompt
Admin can push global prompt to overwrite all user prompts
Prompt changes take effect on next evaluation

title: LLM connection test succeeds but evaluation fails with timeout
description: The connection test endpoint uses a simple ping that succeeds even when the API key has insufficient quota. Actual evaluation requests then fail with a 429 or timeout error, confusing users who believe their connection is working.
acceptance_criteria: Connection test sends a minimal evaluation request, not just an auth check
Test validates quota availability
Clear error message distinguishes auth failure from quota exhaustion

title: Add retry logic with exponential backoff for LLM API calls
description: Implement automatic retry with exponential backoff for transient LLM API errors (rate limits, server errors). Non-retryable errors like auth failures should fail immediately.
acceptance_criteria: Retry up to 3 times with exponential backoff
Retry only on transient errors (429, 500, 503)
Do not retry on auth failures (401, 403)
Log each retry attempt for debugging

title: Add Gemini as a third LLM provider option
description: Extend the LLM integration to support Google Gemini models alongside Claude and OpenAI. This includes API integration, model selection, and connection testing for Gemini.
acceptance_criteria: Gemini appears as a provider option in LLM settings
Support for Gemini Pro and Gemini Ultra models
Connection test works with Google AI API key
Evaluation produces consistent output format across all providers

title: Trim whitespace from pasted API keys during validation
description: When users paste API keys that contain trailing whitespace or newline characters, the validation passes but API calls fail. The key should be trimmed before storage and validation.
acceptance_criteria: API keys are trimmed of whitespace before storage
Leading and trailing whitespace in pasted keys does not cause failures

title: Optimize SQLite query performance for assessment log
description: The assessment log query becomes slow when the log exceeds 10,000 entries. Add indexes, implement pagination, and optimize the query to handle large datasets efficiently within SQLite.
acceptance_criteria: Assessment log loads within 2 seconds for up to 50,000 entries
Pagination with configurable page size (default 50)
Indexes added on frequently queried columns
Query execution plan verified with EXPLAIN

title: Optimize SQLite WAL checkpoint strategy for concurrent access
description: The current SQLite WAL mode configuration uses default checkpointing, which can cause write starvation under concurrent load. Implement a custom checkpoint strategy to balance read and write performance.
acceptance_criteria: Custom WAL checkpoint runs on configurable interval
Checkpoint does not block active read transactions
WAL file size stays within configured limits

title: Implement batch file upload with progress tracking
description: Allow users to upload multiple files simultaneously for batch evaluation. Display progress indicators for each file showing upload, processing, and evaluation status.
acceptance_criteria: Drag-and-drop or file picker for multiple files
Individual progress bar per file
Overall batch progress indicator
Failed files shown with error details without blocking others

title: Add duplicate file detection in batch uploads
description: Detect and warn users when they upload duplicate files in a batch, either files with the same name or identical content hash. Allow users to skip or re-evaluate duplicates.
acceptance_criteria: Detect duplicate file names within a batch
Detect identical file content via SHA-256 hash
Warning UI with option to skip or proceed
Previously evaluated documents also checked for duplicates

title: PPTX files with embedded videos crash text extraction
acceptance_criteria: PPTX files with embedded media do not crash the extraction pipeline
Non-text content is gracefully skipped with a note in the output
Error is logged with file details for debugging

title: Add OCR capability for image-only PDF documents
description: Integrate an OCR library to extract text from scanned/image-only PDFs. Currently these documents produce empty text content, which results in meaningless risk scores.
acceptance_criteria: Image-only PDFs produce extracted text via OCR
OCR language configurable (default: English)
Processing time indication for OCR operations
Fallback to 'no text extracted' if OCR fails

title: Large Excel files cause memory spike and server crash
description: Uploading Excel files larger than 20MB causes the Node.js process to run out of memory and crash. The file-service loads the entire workbook into memory for text extraction. Need streaming or chunked processing.
acceptance_criteria: Excel files up to 100MB processed without memory crash
File size limit enforced at upload time with clear error
Processing uses streaming where possible

title: Add file type validation at upload endpoint
acceptance_criteria: Only allowed file extensions are accepted
MIME type check in addition to extension check
Clear error message for rejected file types

title: Implement email notification system for review queue events
description: Send email notifications when documents are submitted to the review queue, when documents are assigned for review, and when review decisions are made. Configurable per user.
acceptance_criteria: Email sent on document submission to queue
Email sent to reviewer on document assignment
Email sent to submitter on approve/decline decision
Users can opt out of notifications in settings

title: Implement in-app notification center for real-time alerts
description: Build an in-app notification system that shows real-time alerts for review queue updates, system announcements, and evaluation completions. Notifications appear as a bell icon with unread count in the header.
acceptance_criteria: Bell icon in header with unread count badge
Dropdown panel showing recent notifications
Mark as read/unread functionality
Notification preferences per user

title: Implement regulation document CRUD operations
description: Build the API and UI for creating, reading, updating, and deleting regulation documents. Each document has metadata (title, type, version, effective date) and content.
acceptance_criteria: Create regulation with title, type, content, effective date
List regulations with search and filtering
Edit regulation metadata and content
Soft delete with Active/Inactive status toggle

title: Add version control for regulation documents
description: Track changes to regulation documents with version history. Each edit creates a new version while preserving previous versions for audit and rollback purposes.
acceptance_criteria: Each save creates a new version with timestamp and editor
Version history viewable for each document
Ability to view and compare previous versions
Rollback to any previous version

title: Integrate regulation documents into AI evaluation prompt
description: Include active regulation documents as contextual reference material in the AI evaluation prompt so the LLM can assess documents against specific regulatory requirements rather than general knowledge.
acceptance_criteria: Active regulations are included as context in AI prompts
Users can select which regulations to apply per evaluation
Token count monitoring to avoid exceeding model context limits
Performance impact measured and documented

title: Regulation document preview does not hide Assessment Outcomes column
description: The regulation document preview page incorrectly shows the Assessment Outcomes column from the evaluation view. This column should be hidden when viewing regulation documents since they are reference material, not evaluated content.
acceptance_criteria: Assessment Outcomes column hidden in regulation document preview
All other preview columns render correctly

title: Seed regulation library with initial export control documents
description: Populate the regulation library with an initial set of commonly referenced export control regulations (EAR, ITAR summaries, EU Dual-Use Regulation) as seed data for new deployments.

title: Set up PM2 ecosystem configuration for production
acceptance_criteria: ecosystem.config.js with production settings
Auto-restart on crash with max restart limit
Log rotation configured
Environment variables loaded from .env

title: Create automated deployment scripts for code and full deployments
description: Create deploy-code.sh for code-only updates and deploy-full.sh for complete deployments including data. Scripts should handle SSH, file sync, PM2 restart, and post-deploy verification.
acceptance_criteria: deploy-code.sh pushes code without affecting user data
deploy-full.sh performs complete deployment including data reset
Scripts validate SSH connectivity before deploying
Deployment log created for audit trail

title: Implement SSL/HTTPS setup with Let's Encrypt auto-renewal
description: Configure HTTPS with Let's Encrypt certificates, automated renewal via certbot, and PM2 restart hooks for certificate updates.
acceptance_criteria: SSL certificates obtained via certbot
Auto-renewal cron job configured
PM2 restarts after certificate renewal
HTTP to HTTPS redirect in production

title: Add multi-environment deployment support (DEV/QA/UAT)
description: Support deploying to multiple environments with environment-specific configuration. Each environment has its own server, database, and API keys.
acceptance_criteria: Deployment scripts accept environment parameter
Environment label displayed in application header
Environment-specific .env files supported
Build info shows current environment and version

title: Set up application health check endpoint and monitoring
acceptance_criteria: GET /api/health returns server status, uptime, and version
Database connectivity check included
LLM provider connectivity status included

title: Deploy script fails silently when SSH connection drops
description: The deploy-code.sh script does not properly detect SSH connection failures. If the SSH session drops mid-deploy, the script reports success but the deployment is incomplete, leaving the server in a potentially broken state.
acceptance_criteria: SSH connectivity verified before and after deployment
Deployment script exits with non-zero code on any SSH failure
Rollback procedure triggered on failed deployment

title: Implement database backup and restore procedures
description: Create automated backup scripts for the SQLite database and review queue data. Backups should run on a schedule and be stored in a configurable location with rotation.
acceptance_criteria: Daily automated backup of SQLite database files
Backup stored in configurable directory with date-stamped filenames
Restore script that validates backup integrity before applying
Retention policy: keep last 30 daily backups

title: Add audit logging across all API endpoints
description: Implement comprehensive audit logging that records all API calls with user identity, action, resource, timestamp, and result. This is needed for compliance and security audit requirements across all feature areas.
acceptance_criteria: All API endpoints log: user, action, resource, timestamp, IP, result
Audit log stored in dedicated database table
Audit log searchable by user, date range, action type
Audit log retention policy configurable
Log entries are tamper-resistant (append-only)

title: Implement request rate limiting across all endpoints
description: Add rate limiting to prevent abuse and ensure fair resource allocation. Different rate limits for authenticated vs. unauthenticated requests, and higher limits for admin users.
acceptance_criteria: Rate limits applied to all API endpoints
Configurable limits per role (admin gets higher limits)
429 response with Retry-After header on limit exceeded
Rate limit state stored in memory (not database) for performance

title: Fix assessment log CSV export truncating long reasoning text
description: When exporting the assessment log to CSV, the AI reasoning text is truncated at 500 characters. Long reasoning responses from the LLM are cut off, losing important analysis details in the export.
acceptance_criteria: Full reasoning text included in CSV export
Special characters properly escaped in CSV
File size warning if export exceeds 50MB

title: Add Viewer role with read-only access to evaluation results
description: Add a new Viewer role that can see evaluation results and the review queue but cannot submit documents, perform reviews, or change settings. This is for stakeholders who need visibility without action capability.
acceptance_criteria: New Viewer role added to role definitions
Viewer can see evaluation history and review queue
Viewer cannot submit, evaluate, review, or change settings
Role selectable when creating or editing users

title: Concurrent file uploads overwrite temp files with same name
acceptance_criteria: Each upload uses a unique temporary filename (UUID prefix)
Concurrent uploads from multiple users do not interfere
Temporary files cleaned up after processing

title: Add end-to-end test suite for evaluation workflow
acceptance_criteria: E2E tests cover: login, evaluate text, evaluate file, submit to review, approve
Tests run against a test database with fixture data
CI pipeline integration with pass/fail reporting

title: Refactor frontend to use component-based architecture
description: The current vanilla JS frontend has grown complex with tightly coupled modules. Refactor to a cleaner component-based architecture with better state management, event handling, and testability.

title: Batch evaluation shows wrong file name in results for re-evaluated documents
description: When a user re-evaluates a document from the batch results, the file name displayed in the results panel shows the previous document's name instead of the current one. This appears to be a stale state reference in the frontend.
acceptance_criteria: File name in results always matches the evaluated document
State properly reset between sequential evaluations
Batch results panel accurately reflects current evaluation

title: Implement scheduled batch evaluation for automated processing
description: Allow administrators to configure scheduled batch evaluations that automatically process documents from a designated folder. Documents are picked up on a cron schedule, evaluated, and results are stored in the assessment log.
acceptance_criteria: Admin can configure a watch folder path
Cron-style schedule for pickup interval
Processed documents moved to an archive folder
Results logged and optionally auto-submitted to review queue

title: Build dashboard for real-time evaluation metrics
description: Create a dashboard page showing real-time metrics: evaluations today, average risk score, risk category distribution, LLM response times, and system health indicators. Available to admin and analyst roles.
acceptance_criteria: Dashboard shows evaluations count for today/week/month
Risk category distribution pie chart
Average LLM response time trend
System health indicators (database, LLM connectivity)

title: Document API endpoints with OpenAPI/Swagger specification
acceptance_criteria: OpenAPI 3.0 spec covering all API endpoints
Swagger UI accessible at /api/docs in development
Request/response schemas documented with examples

title: Review queue document preview fails for documents with special characters in filename
description: Documents with special characters (spaces, ampersands, parentheses) in their filename fail to load in the review queue preview. The file path is not properly URL-encoded when constructing the preview request.
acceptance_criteria: File paths properly URL-encoded in preview requests
Documents with spaces, ampersands, and parentheses in names load correctly
Consistent encoding across all endpoints that reference file paths

title: Add dark mode theme for evaluation interface
description: Provide a dark mode toggle in user settings that switches the entire UI to a dark color scheme. Useful for analysts doing extended review sessions in low-light environments.
acceptance_criteria: Dark mode toggle in user settings
All UI components styled for dark mode
Theme preference persists across sessions

title: Session middleware crash for unauthenticated requests to static assets
description: The session middleware throws an unhandled error when processing requests for static assets (CSS, JS, images) from unauthenticated users. This causes intermittent 500 errors on the login page.
acceptance_criteria: Static asset requests bypass session validation
Login page loads reliably without 500 errors
Session middleware handles missing session gracefully

title: Add input sanitization for evaluation text to prevent prompt injection
description: Sanitize user-provided evaluation text to prevent prompt injection attacks that could manipulate the AI into ignoring risk assessment instructions or producing misleading results.
acceptance_criteria: Known prompt injection patterns detected and neutralized
Sanitization does not alter legitimate document content
Injection attempts logged for security review
Configurable sanitization rules

title: Implement export of evaluation results to PDF report
description: Generate a formatted PDF report from evaluation results that includes document metadata, risk score, risk category, AI reasoning, trigger paragraphs, and review status. The report should be downloadable and printable.
acceptance_criteria: PDF report generated from evaluation results
Report includes: metadata, risk score, category, reasoning, triggers
Company logo and branding configurable
Download button available on evaluation results page

title: Add bulk user import from CSV for enterprise deployments
description: Allow administrators to import multiple users at once via CSV upload, with columns for username, role, email, and temporary password. Useful for onboarding large teams.

title: Risk score percentage displayed incorrectly in non-US locales
description: In browser locales that use comma as decimal separator (e.g., de-DE, fr-FR), the risk score percentage is displayed incorrectly. A score of 75.5% might show as 75,5% or the formatting breaks entirely.
acceptance_criteria: Risk score always displayed with period as decimal separator
Consistent number formatting across all locales
No layout breaking from locale-specific number formats

title: Add confirmation dialog before pushing global prompt to all users
description: When an admin clicks 'Push Global Prompt', all user custom prompts are immediately overwritten without a confirmation dialog. This has caused accidental loss of carefully crafted user prompts.
acceptance_criteria: Confirmation dialog shown before pushing global prompt
Dialog shows count of users whose prompts will be overwritten
Option to exclude specific users from the push
